finding_results.notes: Reworked the cited Ren-POV beats so Jet, Faye, and Spike are read through visible cues, tone, and Ren's marked guesses rather than narrator-certainty. Also cleaned adjacent overlay lines in the same scene to keep the camera consistently behind Ren's eyes.

Collapsed the parking spot, Takara bounty, old ping, and market contact into one confrontation where Ren explains the bait as a single delivery mechanism, adds the cost of six months and prepaid berth money, and turns the scene back toward Jet's partner instead of auditing clues one by one.

Four Ren speeches revised to stay in her concrete/logistical register. (1) Line 307: 'I needed you to touch the part that belonged to you' (poetic thesis) replaced with tactical method explanation — 'I needed you to find your own piece first. Your locker, the precinct file — so when the rest showed up, you'd know your part wasn't forged.' (2) Line 411: Removed thesis-cap sentence 'It wasn't one thing.' — the enumeration of specific acts now ends on the concrete accusation and flows into line 415's personal admission. (3) Line 425: 'It wasn't design. It was that mess, over and over, and you still all wound up under one roof' replaced with 'Nobody designed that.' plus a thumbnail-rubbing pause and 'You just kept showing up in the same records. Same port, same—roof.' — visible self-correction at the em-dash where she catches herself reaching for domestic language; Jet's 'My what' reaction preserved. (4) Line 531: Thematic frame ('I got tired of them living only in forms' / 'That's what I wanted in the room') replaced with halting delivery — 'I just—' going straight into sensory details, then a self-correction: 'That's—I wanted them to have that. Not just the intake numbers.' Two beats now include visible self-corrections (lines 425 and 531). None of the four revised passages can be lifted as standalone theses about the crew or the system.

finding_results.notes: Removed the repeated exact cue 'There it was' from the chapter and replaced the cited turn beats with distinct physical or kinetic actions, including Jet's reaction to the wall and his contact with the working-copy bundles.

pass_summaries.summary: Addressed the close-third POV overlay in Ren's scene and rebuilt the late reveal so the bait logic lands as one escalating exchange instead of a checklist recap.

Single finding addressed: Ren's four polished thematic mini-speeches revised into her style-bible register of calm mechanical precision with concrete/logistical vocabulary. Poetic thesis statements replaced with tactical explanations, personal admissions of failed search, fragmented trailing speech, and visible self-corrections. Surrounding reactions (Jet's 'My what,' Ren's blush, Spike's manifest focus) all preserved. No new continuity contradictions introduced. Heading contract intact.
Chapter 16's revelation beats now land through scene-specific motion and pressure instead of repeated formula cues or stock look/glance staging.

summary: Merged three-pass revision report (FIXED=4, PARTIAL=0, UNRESOLVED=0).
